evidence_for: Minimum cluster share is 14.3%, so no segment is tiny.
evidence_against: Silhouette is 0.124 and Davies-Bouldin is 2.267, weaker than k=5.
recommendation: Not preferred; useful as a simpler fallback only.

evidence_for: Best baseline silhouette at 0.135; largest cluster share is 32.8%; mean seed ARI is 0.998.
evidence_against: Smallest cluster share is 6.8%, so one segment is relatively small and needs business interpretation.
recommendation: Still supported as the practical final candidate, with k=6 noted as the closest alternative.

evidence_for: Largest cluster share falls to 29.8% and seed stability is very high, with mean ARI 0.999.
evidence_against: Silhouette falls to 0.127 and Calinski-Harabasz is lower than k=5.
recommendation: Worth reviewing in the final discussion, but not enough evidence to replace k=5 now.
